title: Epoxy Acids of Mucunα pruritα Seed Oil
author: S. Q. HASAN
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Aligarh Muslim University, Aligarh (India) 

  Manuscript received 26 March 1979, revised 6 September 1979, accepted 9 July 1980  

 Oil from the seed of  \(Mucuna\)   \(prurita\)  (Leguminosae, Hindi, —Jangli Kawanch) contains preliously unidentified  \(cis\) -12, 13-epoxyoctudec- \(trans\) -9-enoic (1%) and the more common vernolic lets-12, 13-epaxyoctadec- \(cis\) -9-enoic] (4%) acid. Gunstone's procedure of direct acetolysis of oil containing epoxy acids in minor amounts was adopted to characterize the epoxy acids in the oil. The techniques used in isolation and identification of the acids included thin-layer and column chromatography, IR, UV, NMR and chemical reactions ( \(viz\) ., reduction and oxidative cleavage) coupled with GLC.
